input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI development on macOS targeting iOS and Android.

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS builds)

**Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode)

- Install the latest stable Xcode (currently Xcode 16.x for iOS 18 SDK).
- After install, run:
  ```
  xcode-select --install        # command-line tools
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version` should print the version and build number.

---

## 2. .NET SDK

**Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — download the latest .NET 8 (LTS) or .NET 9 SDK for macOS (Arm64 for Apple Silicon, x64 for Intel).

- The installer is a `.pkg` file. Run it.
- **Discover versions:** The download page always shows the current recommended SDK. For MAUI, use the latest patch of the current LTS or STS release.
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version` and `dotnet --list-sdks`

---

## 3. .NET MAUI Workload

Installed via the .NET CLI — no separate download needed:

```bash
dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and platform dependencies.
- **Discover what's available:** `dotnet workload search maui`
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` as installed.

---

## 4. Android SDK, NDK & Emulator

The MAUI workload installs the **Android SDK** automatically under `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx` (or via `$ANDROID_HOME`).

If you need to manage it manually or want an emulator:

**Option A — Android Studio (recommended for emulator/device management):**
- **Source:** [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)
- Use its SDK Manager to install required API levels, build-tools, and emulator images.

**Option B — CLI only:**
- The MAUI build auto-provisions what it needs. For explicit control:
  ```bash
  dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies \
    -f net8.0-android \
    "-p:AndroidSdkDirectory=$HOME/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx" \
    -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
  ```

**Environment variables** (add to `~/.zshrc`):
```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx"
export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$ANDROID_HOME/emulator:$PATH"
```

**Verify:**
- `adb --version`
- `emulator -list-avds` (if using an emulator)

---

## 5. OpenJDK (Android builds require it)

The MAUI workload bundles a Microsoft OpenJDK. If you need to install it explicitly:

**Source:** [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download) — Microsoft Build of OpenJDK 17.

- **Verify:** `java -version` → should show version 17.x.

---

## 6. (Optional) IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code + C# Dev Kit** | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) + install the **C# Dev Kit** and **.NET MAUI** extensions |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) (has built-in MAUI support) |

> Visual Studio for Mac has been retired. VS Code with C# Dev Kit is now the official Microsoft path on macOS.

---

## 7. End-to-End Verification

```bash
# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>
```

To find simulator UDIDs: `xcrun simctl list devices available`

---

## Quick Checklist

```
☐  Xcode installed + license accepted
☐  .NET SDK installed (dotnet --version works)
☐  MAUI workload installed (dotnet workload list shows maui)
☐  Android SDK present + licenses accepted
☐  JDK 17 available (java -version)
☐  IDE of choice configured
☐  dotnet new maui template builds for both platforms
```

This covers everything from a clean machine to a verified working MAUI environment.
